description: This figure presents a corrosion schematic diagram illustrating the electrochemical degradation mechanisms of Fe–30Mn–8Al–C low-density steel (LDS) with varying Cu contents (0, 1, and 3 wt%) in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution. The diagram is organized into three rows representing 0Cu, 1Cu, and 3Cu alloys, each showing early-stage microstructure (carbide distribution), late-stage ionic interactions (Cl⁻, Cu²⁺, Fe²⁺, Mn²⁺, Al³⁺ adsorption), and corresponding corroded surface morphologies. For 0Cu, carbides attach to the austenite matrix surface causing lattice distortion and slight pitting. In 1Cu, Cu addition refines carbides, creating numerous potential difference sites between carbides and matrix, resulting in multiple pits. For 3Cu, excessive Cu remains undissolved and enriches at grain boundaries, generating synergistic microgalvanic corrosion with carbides and austenite, leading to severe intergranular corrosion. The bottom lists oxidation reactions for Fe, Al, Mn, and Cu, supporting the proposed mechanism that Cu addition progressively deteriorates corrosion resistance.
